computer.name: AZL-SEC Node
computer.description: The station's primary security terminal. Controls door locks, camera feeds, and alarm systems. Three subsystems: a primary console (visible from any approach), a wireless relay tucked in the rafters, and a hidden black-budget log the GM only reveals once a PC succeeds at the wireless DC 28 hack.
computer.successDescription: You gain access to security camera feeds and can unlock doors remotely for the next hour.
computer.criticalSuccessDescription: You also disable the station's alarm system and gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Stealth checks for the duration.
computer.failureDescription: The session times out and you're logged off. Future attempts on the same console take a -2 circumstance penalty for 10 minutes.

accessPoints.name: Primary Console (Front Desk)

countermeasures.name: System Lockout
countermeasures.description: The terminal locks out the user for 10 minutes. An alarm sounds alerting nearby guards.

countermeasures.name: Active Trace
countermeasures.description: Each round the trace continues, the security system narrows the hacker's location. After 3 rounds, station guards know exactly where the PCs are.

accessPoints.notes: GM: a security guard is stationed 30 ft away. They notice on a successful Perception DC 22 (modified by ambient noise — busy market = +5).

accessPoints.name: Wireless Relay (Rafters)
accessPoints.notes: GM: spotting the relay requires a Perception DC 24 from the floor. Athletics DC 18 to climb up; Acrobatics DC 22 to do it quietly.

accessPoints.name: Black Budget Log

countermeasures.name: Mind Spike Hex
countermeasures.description: On the first failure, a magical feedback hex deals 4d6 mental damage to the hacker (basic Will save DC 28). Critical failure also stuns 1.

accessPoints.notes: GM: this subsystem stays hidden (and absent from player view) until a PC successfully hacks the Wireless Relay. Then reveal it manually via the eye toggle on the GM canvas.
